---
title: Bash Profiles
subtitle: "Customizing Bash terminals with `~/.bash_profile` or `~/.bashrc`"
---

::: {.callout-important}
Bash shell has been replaced with Zsh in Mac OS. The information on this page will no longer modify the Mac Terminal window. See [Zsh](zsh.qmd) for customizing Mac Terminal behavior.
:::

Use a `~/.bash_profile` file (preferred) or a `~/.bashrc` file to configure the appearance and behavior of Bash shell and terminals. More information can be found [here](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Bash/Prompt_customization).

### Shebang

Specify that the script should be run using the Bash shell found in the user's environment.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
```

### Git Branch

To get the current working branch of a Git repository:

```bash
git_branch() {
    git branch 2>/dev/null | sed -e '/^[^*]/d' -e 's/* \(.*\)/ (\1)/'
}
```

### Terminal Prompt

#### `PS1` Letter codes

| Code | Description                                                          |
|:----:|:---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| \\a | an ASCII bell character (07) |
| \\d | date in "Weekday Month Date" format (*e.g.*, "Tue May 26") |
| \\D{format} | format is passed to strftime(3) and the result is inserted into the prompt string; an empty format results in a locale-specific time representation. The braces are required |
| \\e | an ASCII escape character (033) |
| \\h | hostname up to the first "." |
| \\H | hostname |
| \\j | number of jobs currently managed by the shell |
| \\l | basename of the shell’s terminal device name |
| \\n | newline |
| \\r | carriage return |
| \\s | name of the shell, the basename of $0 (the portion following the final slash) |
| \\t | current time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format |
| \\T | current time in 12-hour HH:MM:SS format |
| \\@ | current time in 12-hour am/pm format |
| \\A | current time in 24-hour HH:MM format |
| \\u | username of the current user |
| \\v | version of bash (e.g., 2.00) |
| \\V | release of bash, version + patch level (e.g., 2.00.0) |
| \\w | current working directory, with $HOME abbreviated with a tilde |
| \\W | basename of the current working directory, with $HOME abbreviated with a tilde |
| \\\! | history number of this command |
| \\\# | command number of this command |
| \\\$ | if the effective UID is 0, a #, otherwise a $ |
| \\nnn | the character corresponding to the octal number nnn |
| \\\\ | a backslash |
| \\\[ | begin a sequence of non-printing characters, which could be used to embed a terminal control sequence into the prompt |
| \\\] | end a sequence of non-printing characters |
: Letter codes for `PS1` bash prompts

#### Colors

Colors and text formatting syntax can be found [here](https://misc.flogisoft.com/bash/tip_colors_and_formatting). To see samples and codes of the supported colors:

```bash
for C in {0..255}; do
    tput setab $C
    echo -n "$C "
done
tput sgr0
echo
```

### Putting it all together

```{.bash filename="~/.bash_profile"}
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# GIT FUNCTIONS
git_branch() {
    git branch 2>/dev/null | sed -e '/^[^*]/d' -e 's/* \(.*\)/ (\1)/'
}

# TERMINAL PROMPT
PS1="\[\e[0;93m\]\u\[\e[m\]"                 # username
PS1+=" "                                     # space for readability
PS1+="\[\e[0;95m\]\W\[\e[m\]"                # PWD, current directory only
PS1+="\[\e[0;92m\]\$(git_branch)\[\e[m\]"    # current branch
PS1+=" "    # space
PS1+=">> "    # end prompt
export PS1;

export CLICOLOR=1
export LSCOLORS=ExFxBxDxCxegedabagacad
```

## Command Aliases

Shortcuts and abbreviated commands to accelerate prompting. These could optionally be included in their own `~/.aliases` file.

### Moving up directory chain

```{.bash filename="~/.aliases"}
alias ..="cd .."
alias ...="cd ../.."
alias ....="cd ../../.."
alias .....="cd ../../../.."
```

### Navigate to common directories

```bash
alias dl="cd ~/Downloads"
alias dt="cd ~/Desktop"
alias dc="cd ~/Documents"
alias home="cd ~"
```

### Minimize accidental deletion

Force a confirmation prompt before removing (`rm`) or overwriting (`mv`) a file. Without these aliases, both commands just happen.

```bash
alias rm="rm -i"
alias mv="mv -i"
```

### Color coded `ls` with slash

This modifies the printout from `ls` to color-code files and directories and to include a leading forward-slash on directories for clarity.

```bash
alias ls="ls -FG"
```

### Login shortcuts

```bash
alias ud="ssh -X grossi@biohen.dbi.udel.edu"
```

## Using multiple files

Assume prompt settings are in `~/.bash-prompt` and aliases are in `~/.aliases`, use a separate `~/.bash_profile` to point to these files:

```{.bash filename="~/.bash_profile"}
source ~/.bash_prompt
source ~/.aliases

echo PATH=$PATH:~/homebrew/bin
```

## After updating any of these files

After updating any these files, either restart the terminal or use `source <filename>` to load the new settings.
